page: Microsoft Azure NV28adms_V710_v5 is an x86_64 architecture virtual machine belonging to the NVadsV710v5 server family. Powered by dedicated AMD processors, this instance is managed via the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 28 dedicated vCPUs paired with 160.0 GB of system memory, yielding a memory-to-core ratio of 5.71 GB per vCPU. The hardware configuration is bundled with a single V710 GPU providing 22 GB of dedicated VRAM, alongside 1649 GB of local storage. The instance is also premium storage capable but does not include complimentary public IPv4 addresses.

From a resource density perspective, the server offers a balanced allocation of compute, memory, and graphics acceleration. The inclusion of a dedicated V710 GPU with 22 GB VRAM makes it a specialized option for workloads requiring hardware acceleration without needing ultra-high-end enterprise GPUs. Qualitatively, the hardware profile represents a mid-to-high tier resource density, combining substantial local storage capacity with a memory-intensive footprint. A notable tradeoff is the lack of complimentary public IPv4 addresses, which may require additional network configuration or costs for external connectivity. Additionally, no public benchmark data is available for this specific instance type, meaning performance characteristics must be evaluated based on raw hardware specifications.

The NV28adms_V710_v5 is designed for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated workloads. Appropriate use cases include virtual desktop infrastructure, 3D visualization, medium-scale machine learning inference, and graphics-heavy applications. The combination of 160.0 GB of RAM and 1649 GB of local storage also makes it suitable for database hosting and data processing tasks that benefit from local scratch space and GPU acceleration.
description: The Microsoft Azure NV28adms_V710_v5 is an x86_64 virtual machine from the NVadsV710v5 family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 28 dedicated AMD vCPUs, 160.0 GB of system memory, and a single V710 GPU with 22 GB of VRAM. Local storage is supported by 1649 GB of disk space, and the instance is premium storage capable. Designed for memory-intensive and GPU-accelerated tasks, this server provides a balanced resource density for workloads requiring dedicated graphics processing and substantial memory. Tradeoffs include the absence of complimentary public IPv4 addresses and a lack of public benchmark data. It is suited for virtual desktop infrastructure, 3D rendering, database hosting, and medium-scale machine learning inference workloads. The instance offers a memory-to-core ratio of 5.71 GB per vCPU, supporting memory-intensive applications.
